title: Nova Biomedical StatStrip Blood Glucose Meter - Strip Not Recognized or Insertion Errors
description: Troubleshooting strip detection failures and insertion errors on the StatStrip blood glucose meter.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not troubleshoot while actively testing a patient sample. Move testing to another meter if available.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error Message
steps.instructions: Power on the device and insert a test strip. Confirm the exact error (e.g., “Strip Not Recognized” or insertion error).

steps.title: Check Test Strip Compatibility and Lot
steps.instructions: Ensure the strips are correct for the StatStrip system and not mixed with another model or vendor. Verify correct strip type and confirm lot is approved for use.

steps.title: Inspect Strip Expiration and Storage Conditions
steps.instructions: Check expiration date and confirm strips were stored per manufacturer guidelines (sealed container, proper temperature/humidity).

steps.title: Inspect Strip Insertion Technique
steps.instructions: Insert the strip fully and in the correct orientation. Ensure contacts are facing the correct direction and avoid partial insertion.

steps.title: Check for Contamination or Debris in Strip Port
steps.instructions: Visually inspect the strip port for dirt, dried fluid, or obstructed contacts. Clean gently per approved methods.

steps.title: Try a New Test Strip from a Different Vial
steps.instructions: Insert a strip from a new vial or different lot to determine if the issue is strip-related.

steps.title: Check Meter Battery and Power Stability
steps.instructions: Ensure the battery is adequately charged or properly seated. Replace or reseat if needed.

steps.title: Restart the Device
steps.instructions: Power cycle the meter and attempt strip insertion again.

steps.title: Test with Known Good Conditions
steps.instructions: Use a known good strip, proper insertion, and confirmed good battery to verify consistent recognition.

CCR.Complaint: Meter not recognizing test strips during routine glucose testing.
CCR.Cause: Contaminated strip port preventing proper contact with test strips.
CCR.Resolution: Cleaned strip port and verified proper strip recognition with new vial of strips.

helpfulDetails: Final device status (returned to service or sent for repair)

title: Nova Biomedical StatStrip Blood Glucose Meter - Connectivity or Download Failure
description: Failure to transmit results, docking issues, or inability to download data due to network, docking, or configuration problems.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not delay critical glucose testing due to connectivity issues. Use another functioning meter if needed and manually document results per policy.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Confirm the exact symptom such as meter not uploading results, dock not recognizing meter, wireless icon errors, or data stuck in queue.

steps.title: Check Docking Station Connection (Most Common Cause)
steps.instructions: Ensure dock has power, Ethernet is connected, and try a known working port. Dock the meter and observe syncing behavior.

steps.title: Inspect Meter Dock Contacts
steps.instructions: Check contacts for dirt, corrosion, or damage and clean if needed. Re-dock and test.

steps.title: Check Network Availability
steps.instructions: Verify Ethernet link lights and network access or confirm wireless settings if applicable.

steps.title: Restart the Meter
steps.instructions: Power cycle the meter and re-dock after reboot.

steps.title: Check for Stored Data Backlog
steps.instructions: Attempt manual upload or sync and observe for error messages.

steps.title: Verify Configuration / Device Assignment
steps.instructions: Confirm correct unit ID, system registration, and that the device is active in middleware.

steps.title: Swap with Known Working Components
steps.instructions: Test with another dock or meter to isolate whether the issue follows the device or docking station.

CCR.Complaint: StatStrip meter not uploading glucose results to system when docked.
CCR.Cause: Faulty docking station Ethernet connection preventing network communication.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced docking station and verified successful data upload.

helpfulDetails: Dock power and network status verified
Final device status (working, removed from service, sent for repair)

title: Nova Biomedical StatStrip Blood Glucose Meter - QC Lockout or Control Failures
description: Troubleshooting QC lockout conditions preventing testing due to failed, expired, or incomplete quality control checks.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not attempt patient testing while the meter is in QC lockout. Obtain another functioning meter for patient care.

steps.title: Confirm QC Lockout Message
steps.instructions: Power on the meter and verify the displayed message such as QC required, QC failed, or lockout warning.

steps.title: Check QC Due Status and Timing
steps.instructions: Navigate to QC status, check last successful QC date and time, and compare with facility QC policy.

steps.title: Verify QC Materials
steps.instructions: Inspect QC solution and test strips for expiration, proper storage, and correct QC level.

steps.title: Perform QC Test Properly
steps.instructions: Insert strip correctly, apply QC solution properly, and avoid contamination.

steps.title: Check Strip Lot and Coding
steps.instructions: Ensure strips match expected lot, verify vial matches configuration, and confirm no strip damage.

steps.title: Inspect Meter Contacts and Strip Port
steps.instructions: Check for debris or contamination and clean per approved procedure if needed.

steps.title: Check Docking and Connectivity
steps.instructions: Ensure proper docking and confirm communication with data system if QC upload is required.

steps.title: Restart the Meter
steps.instructions: Power cycle the device after successful QC to clear lockout.

CCR.Complaint: Meter in QC lockout and unable to perform patient testing.
CCR.Cause: Expired QC solution causing repeated QC failures and lockout condition.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced QC materials, performed successful QC, and restored meter to service.

helpfulDetails: Meter docking and upload status confirmed
Final device status (returned to service or removed)

title: Nova Biomedical StatStrip Glucose Monitor System - Battery Not Charging or Docking Failure
description: Meter not charging, low battery despite docking, or no charging indicator when placed in base station.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the meter from clinical use if battery is critically low or unreliable. Use a backup device for patient testing. Expected: No patient care interruption Why it matters: Power loss during testing can delay care or cause inaccurate documentation

steps.title: Verify Charging Indicator Behavior
steps.instructions: Place the StatStrip in the docking station and observe for charging indicators (LED or on-screen icon). Expected: Charging indicator appears within a few seconds Why it matters: Confirms whether the device recognizes the dock and is receiving power

steps.title: Check Power to Docking Station
steps.instructions: Ensure the docking station is plugged in and powered. Verify outlet functionality using another device. Expected: Dock has confirmed power Why it matters: A dead outlet or unplugged dock is a common and easily missed cause

steps.title: Inspect Docking Station Connections
steps.instructions: Check the power adapter connection to the dock and wall. Look for loose or damaged cables. Expected: All connections secure and intact Why it matters: Intermittent or failed connections prevent consistent charging

steps.title: Clean Charging Contacts (Common Issue)
steps.instructions: Inspect and clean the metal charging contacts on both the meter and the docking station using approved wipes (e.g., alcohol). Expected: Contacts are clean, dry, and free of residue Why it matters: Glucose meters frequently accumulate debris that blocks electrical contact

steps.title: Reseat the Meter in the Dock
steps.instructions: Remove and firmly reseat the meter in the docking station. Ensure it is properly aligned and fully seated. Expected: Charging indicator activates Why it matters: Misalignment prevents proper contact engagement

steps.title: Try a Known Good Dock or Meter
steps.instructions: Place the meter in a different working dock, or place a known working meter in the current dock. Expected: If meter charges in another dock → original dock is faulty If another meter fails in this dock → dock or power supply issue Why it matters: Isolates whether the issue is with the meter or docking station

steps.title: Check for Battery Age or Degradation
steps.instructions: If the meter only holds a charge briefly or fails to charge consistently, the internal battery may be worn. Expected: Battery maintains charge during normal use Why it matters: Rechargeable batteries degrade over time and eventually fail

steps.title: Restart the Meter
steps.instructions: Power cycle the device and return it to the dock. Expected: Charging resumes after reboot Why it matters: Clears minor software or charging recognition issues

CCR.Complaint: StatStrip meter not charging in docking station; battery remains low.
CCR.Cause: Dirty charging contacts preventing electrical connection.
CCR.Resolution: Cleaned contacts and verified proper charging function in dock.

helpfulDetails: Results with alternate dock or meter
Final device status (returned to service or sent out)

title: Nova Biomedical StatStrip Blood Glucose Meter - Display or Button Errors
description: Troubleshooting unresponsive buttons, display issues, or frozen screens on the StatStrip meter.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the device from active patient use and transition testing to a backup meter to avoid delays in care.

steps.title: Inspect for Physical Damage
steps.instructions: Check the screen and keypad for cracks, fluid intrusion, or visible wear. If damage is present, remove from service.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Device
steps.instructions: Turn the meter off, wait 10 seconds, and turn it back on. If resolved, return to service.

steps.title: Check Battery Status and Connections
steps.instructions: Ensure the battery is properly seated and charged. Replace with a known-good battery if needed.

steps.title: Inspect and Clean the Keypad Area
steps.instructions: Look for dried fluids, glucose residue, or debris around buttons. Clean using approved disinfectant wipes.

steps.title: Test Button Responsiveness
steps.instructions: Press each button individually and observe response on screen to determine if issue is isolated or systemic.

steps.title: Check Screen Functionality
steps.instructions: Observe for dim display, missing segments, flickering, or distortion. Adjust brightness/settings if available.

steps.title: Verify Software/Interface Behavior
steps.instructions: Navigate menus to confirm normal operation and identify software or hardware issues.

steps.title: Check Docking/Charging Station
steps.instructions: Ensure proper docking and communication if the unit syncs or charges via base.

CCR.Complaint: Glucose meter screen freezing and buttons not responding during use.
CCR.Cause: Buttons intermittently unresponsive due to contamination buildup around keypad.
CCR.Resolution: Cleaned keypad and verified proper button function; device returned to service.

helpfulDetails: Final device status (in service or removed)
